steps.title: Distribute the negative sign
steps.content:
When subtracting the entire expression, distribute the negative sign to both terms in the second parentheses:
$$-1(4c^3 - 6) = -4c^3 + 6$$
So: $(9c + 8) - (4c^3 - 6) = 9c + 8 - 4c^3 + 6$

steps.title: Combine like terms
steps.content:
Group terms by type: the $c^3$ term is $-4c^3$, the $c$ term is $9c$, and combine the constants $8 + 6 = 14$.
$$-4c^3 + 9c + 14$$
That matches choice D — D is correct.
